chief_complaint: Transient neurological symptoms (resolved) with melena in elderly hypertensive male
clinical_reasoning: 72-year-old male with transient visual disturbance and confusion (now fully resolved) and dark stools suggestive of melena. Vitals stable: HR 98 (mildly elevated, consistent with anxiety or mild GI blood loss), BP 152/88, SpO2 97%, temperature normal. No active neurological deficit on presentation. The transient symptoms are consistent with TIA — resolved within minutes. Dark stools suggest possible upper GI bleeding, potentially aspirin-related. Both symptom complexes are urgent but currently stable. Route to TIA pathway and GI workup. YELLOW — urgent but stable.
vital_sign_concerns: HR 98 — mildly elevated, may reflect mild hypovolaemia from GI blood loss or anxiety
BP 152/88 — hypertensive, consistent with known poorly controlled hypertension
